summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all major requirements: SHA-256 hashing, directory comparison (all four statuses), sync plan generation, dry-run and execute modes, and an end-to-end integration pipeline including an idempotency check. Edge cases are well-represented — empty files, empty directories, nested paths, missing source files during execute, and the post-sync re-comparison to verify convergence. Design is excellent: a clean MockFileSystem abstraction eliminates all disk I/O, tests are small and focused, fixtures are reusable (BuildFs helper, BuildMixedScenario), and each test has a clear single responsibility with descriptive names following the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Minor gaps: no test for malformed/invalid paths, no concurrent-access scenario, and the Skipped counter in SyncResult is never exercised. These are minor omissions in an otherwise thorough, well-structured suite.
